summary: Current main contains the first managed before_execution restore slice (merged as 02631dcd6 / PR #3287) and the earlier recovery decision contract (86c2629fb / PR #3280), including frozen-route validation, deterministic restore identity, verified destination evidence, launch blocking, and lineage. The workflow-owned contract currently authorizes only before_execution -> rerun_failed_step; after_execution, after_gate, before_publication, and before_recovery_restoration orchestration are absent, and independent restoration ledger/summary/incident projection plus full restart/replay coverage are incomplete. New code work is required.

requirements.description: Explicit checkpoint-recovery state machine rather than a metadata-only marker.
Preflight validates action, phase, boundary, kind, capability snapshot, restore route, and side-effect safety.
Restore route is resolved from the frozen capability snapshot through the activity catalog.
Destination agent-run identity and restore idempotency key are deterministic and recorded before restore.
Recovered agents require a verified destination locator and restoration evidence before launch.
Source locator and source absolute path are never used as the destination workspace.
before_execution restores and runs the failed step exactly once as a new Step Execution.
after_execution resumes at gate/candidate phase without rerunning mutation.
after_gate continues after an accepted gate without rerunning implementation or verification.
before_publication resumes publication without rerunning implementation or verification.
before_recovery_restoration retries restoration without creating a business Step Execution.
Unknown or contradictory boundary/phase combinations fail closed.
Earlier accepted steps are preserved as provenance and are not rerun, only after restoration validation.
New semantic work receives correct local identity and cross-run lineage.
Restoration retries do not increment business Step Execution ordinals.
Side-effect dispositions remain authoritative after restore.
Restoration outcome is independently visible in ledger, run summary, and incident evidence.
Workflow/worker restarts and duplicate requests reconcile idempotently.
Existing Temporal histories replay across explicit patch/version cutovers.
